stations.name: Morningside railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closest station for the eastern Seven Hills streets. Walk time depends on which ridge you live on.

stations.name: Coorparoo railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closer for the southern Seven Hills streets near Old Cleveland Road.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 12 to 18 minutes to Central station off-peak from either station. Verify on Translink.

buses.note: Bus routes through Seven Hills run the Old Cleveland Road and Stanley Street East corridors. Routes change with Translink service updates.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge and the M3 corridor for southern travel. Gateway Motorway via Cannon Hill for northern routes.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 15 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak via the Story Bridge or Stanley Street.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_peak_note: Peak commute typically extends to 20 to 30 minutes.

connections: Seven Hills Bushland Reserve walking and mountain bike trails
Norman Creek bikeway connecting to the riverside path system
bike_walk.note: Topography is hilly. Casual cyclists often plan routes around the steeper grades.

secondary_state.name: Catchment varies by street
secondary_state.note: Different Seven Hills streets fall within different secondary catchments. Use the QGSO lookup to confirm for any address.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Seven Hills itself is residential. Everyday dining and retail run through the Camp Hill Marketplace, the Coorparoo Square precinct, and the Morningside village strip on Wynnum Road. Bulimba and Hawthorne dining are within seven to ten minutes by car.
lifestyle.topography_note: Seven Hills is named for its elevated terrain. Many streets sit on ridges with western or northern aspects, and the views from those elevated blocks are part of what defines the suburb's character.

pockets.name: Ridge streets
pockets.character: Elevated blocks with city or district outlooks. Premium pocket. Character queenslanders and architect-designed contemporary builds.

pockets.name: Bushland edge
pockets.character: Streets bordering Seven Hills Bushland Reserve. Privacy, established trees, and walking-track access from the doorstep.

pockets.name: Old Cleveland Road corridor
pockets.character: Closer to the arterial. Convenient for transport but trades on a different value proposition than the ridge streets.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers moving up from Coorparoo, Norman Park, or Morningside who want the topography, the views, and the larger blocks Seven Hills tends to offer.
Parents looking for the school catchment corridor combined with the bushland reserve access. The combination is genuinely rare in inner Brisbane.
Buyers attracted to character stock with renovation potential or to the architect-designed contemporary builds that have become more common on the ridge streets.

verify_flags: Exact primary and secondary school catchments
Specific bus route numbers and frequencies
Private school proximity and enrolment availability
